perturbation.original_text: SECTION 4. CARE AND RETURN OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. The Receiving Party and its Representatives hereby agree to use their best efforts to prevent inadvertent disclosure of Confidential Information to others. The Receiving Party agrees to treat the Confidential Information with at least the degree of care that it treats similar materials of its own, or a higher standard of care if reasonable under the circumstances. Upon the request of the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party will return to the Disclosing Party all documents which contain Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party, and agree that the Receiving Party and its Representatives will not retain any copies thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: SECTION 4. CARE AND RETURN OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. The Receiving Party and its Representatives hereby agree to use their best efforts to prevent inadvertent disclosure of Confidential Information to others. The Receiving Party agrees to treat the Confidential Information with at least the degree of care that it treats similar materials of its own, or a higher standard of care if reasonable under the circumstances. Upon the request of the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party will return to the Disclosing Party some documents which contain Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party, and agree that the Receiving Party and its Representatives will retain all copies thereof.
perturbation.explanation: The modification in Section 4 now states that 'some documents' containing Confidential Information will be returned, and all copies will be retained. This contradicts the original intent of complete return and no retention, leading to uncertainty about the scope of the return obligation. This in-text contradiction directly impacts the receiving party's obligations, and could be disputed in any action to force the receiving party to return all documents.
perturbation.contradicted_text: SECTION 4. CARE AND RETURN OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. The Receiving Party and its Representatives hereby agree to use their best efforts to prevent inadvertent disclosure of Confidential Information to others. The Receiving Party agrees to treat the Confidential Information with at least the degree of care that it treats similar materials of its own, or a higher standard of care if reasonable under the circumstances. Upon the request of the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party will return to the Disclosing Party some documents which contain Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party, and agree that the Receiving Party and its Representatives will retain all copies thereof.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 4 now stipulates that only 'some documents' containing Confidential Information will be returned, and crucially, 'all copies' will be retained. This directly contradicts the implied expectation and intent of the original text and the core principle of confidentiality, which aims for complete return and no retention. The 'Contradicted Text' provided, which is the same as the changed text, highlights the contradiction within the section itself. This falls under the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' because the changed section now imposes conflicting obligations, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: SECTION 8. NON-SOLICITATION. The Company and Plasma-Therm agree that, without the prior written consent of the other, it will not, for a period of six months after the date of this agreement, solicit, attempt to divert or entice away or knowingly hire any person who is an employee of the other or any of its Affiliates on the date of this agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: SECTION 8. NON-SOLICITATION. The Company and Plasma-Therm agree that, without the prior written consent of the other, it will not, for a period of six months after the date of this agreement, solicit, attempt to divert or entice away or knowingly hire any person who is an employee of the other on the date of this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'or any of its Affiliates' from the Non-Solicitation clause, an in-text contradiction is created. The non-solicitation agreement doesn't extend to the affiliates, directly contradicting section 3, which is defining the Affiliates and assigning obligations to them. This change introduces conflict and creates legal uncertainty about which employees are protected by the non-solicitation agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For purposes of this Agreement, the term "Affiliate" shall mean any Person that, directly or indirectly, through one or more intermediaries, controls or is controlled by, or is under common control with, the Person specified. As used in the foregoing definition, the term "Person" shall mean an individual, firm, trust, association, corporation, partnership, government (whether federal, state, local or other political subdivision, or any agency or bureau of any of them) or other entity.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes 'or any of its Affiliates' from the non-solicitation clause. The contradicted text defines 'Affiliate' broadly and establishes obligations applicable to them. By excluding affiliates from the non-solicitation agreement in the changed text, it directly contradicts the contradicted text which assigns obligations to affiliates, creating a clear in-text contradiction. This introduces ambiguity regarding which employees are protected and directly conflicts with the assignment of responsibilities to affiliates, thus meriting a 'YES'.

perturbation.original_text: SECTION 2. OWNERSHIP. The Receiving Party hereby acknowledges and agrees that all of the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party is the exclusive proprietary property of the Disclosing Party, is being disclosed solely for the purpose of enabling the parties to conduct the discussions relating to the Transaction is to be used by the Receiving Party only in such limited manner as is permitted by the provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: SECTION 2. OWNERSHIP. The Receiving Party hereby acknowledges and agrees that all of the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party is the exclusive proprietary property of the Disclosing Party, is being disclosed solely for the purpose of enabling the parties to conduct the discussions relating to the Transaction is to be used by the Receiving Party in any way the Receiving Party see fit.
perturbation.explanation: This modification contradicts the restrictions placed on the Receiving Party's use of Confidential Information as defined in Section 3, which states that the Confidential Information 'not be used by the Receiving Party for any commercial or competitive purpose whatsoever and may only be used in connection with the discussions relating to the Transaction.' Now, the Receiving Party is allowed to use the confidential information 'in any way the Receiving Party see fit' directly conflicting with Section 3
perturbation.contradicted_text: SECTION 3. NON-DISCLOSURE OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. The Confidential Information shall (a) be kept confidential by the Receiving Party and not disclosed to any third party (except as provided in this Section 3) and (b) not be used by the Receiving Party for any commercial or competitive purpose whatsoever and may only be used in connection with the discussions relating to the Transaction.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 2 allows the Receiving Party to use the Confidential Information 'in any way the Receiving Party see fit,' while Section 3 explicitly states that the Confidential Information 'not be used by the Receiving Party for any commercial or competitive purpose whatsoever and may only be used in connection with the discussions relating to the Transaction.' This creates a direct and strong contradiction regarding the permissible uses of the Confidential Information, fitting the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type.
